free_text: Praseodymium-141 Photoneutron Cross Section to 65 MeV
Samples were disks of pure 141Pr 1.9 cm in diameter
and 1.59 mm thick.
4x4 in. well-type NaI(Tl) crystal.
Corrections due to sample nonuniformity were less
than 4%. As measurements were performed at energies
above the (g,2n) and (g,3n) thresholds, these
reactions were measured separately to determine the
appropriate corrections. Maximum correction was 1.5%
at 65 MeV.
BP
The combined errors of the factors
contributing to the integrated cross sections: +-6%.
Yield curves reproducibility contributed an
additional 0.5% to the cross section errors of 5.5%.
Table I, page 734.
The combined errors of the factors
contributing to the integrated cross sections: +-6%.
Yield curves reproducibility contributed an
additional 0.5% to the cross section errors of 5.5%.
Table I, page 734.
0.5-MeV energy increment: Samples were
irradiated for 5 min, one minute was allowed to
elapse during which the sample was removed to the
radioactivity counting system, then the annihilation
photons from 140Pr were counted for 5 min.
Cross section curve was calculated from yield
data taken at 0.5-MeV increments.
The combined errors of the factors
contributing to the absolute cross sections: 5.5%.
The average random errors with counting
statistics.
Experimental error shown  in figure
Fig. 1, page 733.
0.125-MeV energy increment: Samples were
irradiated for 5 min, one minute was allowed to
elapse during which the sample was removed to the
radioactivity counting system, then the annihilation
photons from 140Pr were counted for 5 min.
Cross section curve was calculated from yield
data taken at 0.125-MeV increments.
The combined errors of the factors
contributing to the absolute cross sections: 5.5%.
The average random errors with counting
statistics.
Experimental error shown  in figure
Fig. 2, page 734.
